steps.title: Identify the overlooked role
steps.content: The passage contrasts Zelda Fitzgerald's recognized influence on F. Scott Fitzgerald with the fact that many people do not recognize Zelda as a writer herself. Her novel and short stories show that her literary contribution extends beyond inspiring F. Scott.

steps.title: Match the logical consequence
steps.content: A shifts to other factors that motivated F. Scott, C speculates about how the two writers viewed each other's works, and D focuses on autobiographical reading. The sentence needs the consequence of reducing Zelda to an inspiration. The answer is B.
